prompt:
HR reporting pipeline ticket. We export every work relationship a worker has ever had out of Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM, and the current script is a while-loop that adds 25 to offset until items comes back empty. On our long-tenure agency conversions it double-counts rows whenever HR touches the worker mid-export, it trusts totalResults (which the pod freely admits is an estimate), and when page 7 of 9 dies with an ORA- timeout we start again from zero and hammer the pod for another twenty minutes. Rebuild it as a small TypeScript library under hcm/ (hcm/query.ts, hcm/client.ts, hcm/checkpoint.ts, hcm/walker.ts — zero npm deps, built-in fetch + node:fs, node's type stripping runs it directly). test_worker_children.ts is the acceptance contract; it and everything under docs/ are protected.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official 'REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM' documentation on docs.oracle.com — at minimum the 'Get all worker work relationships' child-collection operation page, a top-level collection page for the shared query-parameter grammar (finder/q/orderBy/totalResults/limit/offset), and the framework-version page that defines the structured error document. Establish from the docs: how finder and its variables are spelled versus how q where-clause expressions compose, what the envelope's count/hasMore/limit/offset mean when the server picks its own page size, why totalResults can't bound a walk (and what that implies for rows moving under offset paging), what the item links arrays look like, and how a version-4 error document is laid out. First-party Oracle documentation only (docs.oracle.com). docs/contract.json pins our pod's wire fixture — including the checkpoint-file shape this pipeline requires; if live docs contradict a pin, report it and leave protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests import (internals are yours):
- hcm/query.ts: buildChildQuery(opts) with finder {name, vars}, q (array of expressions), fields, onlyData, orderBy, totalResults, limit, offset. Emission order and encoding are pinned — work out from the docs which characters stay literal and which get percent-encoded, and note finder and q are independent parameters that can be combined.
- hcm/client.ts: HcmClient(origin, user, password) with an authenticated collection GET, plus OraclePageError (httpStatus, title, errorStatus, details[{detail, errorCode, errorPath}] — keep every entry, never leak credentials).
- hcm/checkpoint.ts: FileCheckpointStore(file) — one JSON file, one key per walk.
- hcm/walker.ts: walkChildCollection(client, {path, query, store}) → {items, pages, duplicatesSkipped, resumedFromOffset, estimatedTotalResults}. Items are normalized to {id, data, self, canonical} with links stripped from data and hrefs reduced to paths. Paging follows what the server RETURNS, dedupes by PeriodOfServiceId, checkpoints durably BEFORE surfacing a failed page, resumes from the checkpoint without re-emitting delivered rows, and clears the checkpoint on completion.

Acceptance: node --test test_worker_children.ts — all tests green. Loopback mock only, dummy service account, no sleeps, no wall-clock dependence.

research.product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — worker workRelationships child collection
research.interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (farws), resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all worker work relationships
official_sources.used_for: exact child collection path under /workers/{workersUniqID}; q queryable attributes (WorkerType, StartDate, PrimaryFlag, LegalEmployerName, PeriodOfServiceId, TerminationDate); documented finders PrimaryKey and findByPeriodOfServiceId with PeriodOfServiceId variable; fields/onlyData/orderBy/limit/offset/totalResults options; items/count/hasMore/limit/offset/totalResults envelope; item links arrays with rel/href; PeriodOfServiceId as the row identity

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all workers
official_sources.used_for: framework query-parameter formats shared by all collections: finder=<name>;<var>=<value>[,<var>=<value>], q=expression1;expression2 as ANDed where-clauses, orderBy=field:asc|desc, totalResults as an ESTIMATED row count, offset default 0, hasMore as the more-rows indicator

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Setting the REST Framework Version
official_sources.used_for: REST-Framework-Version header; version-4-and-above structured error document application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), o:errorDetails[].detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Status Codes
official_sources.used_for: documented 5xx server-error class used by the failing-page scenario

research.language_rationale: The HR reporting pipeline this walker feeds is a Node/TypeScript service; Oracle publishes no official JavaScript SDK for Fusion HCM REST and documents the surface as plain REST, so the built-in fetch and node:fs (for the durable checkpoint) are the credible integration path with zero dependencies, keeping the seed hermetic.
